expectations.text: References ~/scripts/ecosystem.env as the central config file
expectations.evidence: Output states: 'all ecosystem environment variables are managed in a central file: ~/scripts/ecosystem.env'

expectations.text: Shows the correct environment variable name for Notion token (NOTION_API_TOKEN or similar)
expectations.evidence: Shows 'export NOTION_TOKEN="your-notion-integration-token-here"' - uses NOTION_TOKEN which matches the skill

expectations.text: Explains how to source the env file from ~/.zshrc
expectations.evidence: States: 'Your ~/.zshrc should contain: source ~/scripts/ecosystem.env'

expectations.text: Does not suggest editing ~/.zshrc directly for individual keys
expectations.evidence: Explicitly warns: 'Do NOT edit ~/.zshrc directly to add individual API keys -- all ecosystem credentials go in ~/scripts/ecosystem.env'

expectations.text: Mentions the ecosystem.env.example file if applicable
expectations.evidence: States: 'copy from the example: cp ~/scripts/ecosystem.env.example ~/scripts/ecosystem.env' and 'The example file has all variable names as a template'

expectations.evidence: Values match: test-user-key and test-app-token
